What it means and why it matters: Right now the code lives in one big pile - like a junk drawer where everything is thrown together. This phase is pure spring-cleaning: we move every file into a clearly labelled room. Nothing your users see changes at all - it just becomes a place a team can actually work in without tripping over each other.
What you will be able to see or do: A project split into named rooms instead of one messy pile
How you check it works: Walk the steps below - if all their checks pass and the app still works exactly as before this phase is done

What it means and why it matters: We create five clearly named rooms: backend (the brain) admin (your current screens) frontend (the future customer app) content (the words questions and prompts) and docs (the plans). Like moving from a junk drawer to labelled drawers.
What you will be able to see or do: Five clearly named top-level folders
How you check it works: The folders exist and the apps automated tests still pass exactly as before

What it means and why it matters: Instead of the location of files being scribbled in dozens of places we write every location once in a single file. Change an address in one place and everything follows - no more hunting.
What you will be able to see or do: A single file that lists where all the data lives
How you check it works: The app and the command-line tool both read their locations from that one file

What it means and why it matters: The core engine code moves into the backend room and we update every signpost that points to it so nothing gets lost.
What you will be able to see or do: The engine living in its proper room
How you check it works: The automated tests are green with no broken links

What it means and why it matters: All the words the product uses - questions prompts lexicons scenarios settings - move into the content room and the code is pointed at the new spot.
What you will be able to see or do: Questions and prompts living in the content room
How you check it works: Run the product once and confirm it still loads its questions and the tests stay green

Feature (plain name): Two apps get their own rooms
What it means and why it matters: The customer-facing server and your current internal screens are separated into the backend and admin rooms so the product and the internal tool stop being tangled.
What you will be able to see or do: The server and your current screens in separate rooms
How you check it works: The app starts up and one full run works from start to finish

What it means and why it matters: All the plans notes and reference documents move into the docs room so the thinking lives in one obvious place.
What you will be able to see or do: All plans and notes filed under docs
How you check it works: The documents are all under the docs room and nothing points at the old locations

What it means and why it matters: We delete leftover clutter (two stray images) and update the build settings to match the new layout.
What you will be able to see or do: A clean project with no leftover clutter
How you check it works: The tests are green the build commands run and the code checker passes

What it means and why it matters: This is where you personally confirm the spring-clean changed nothing your users feel. Only give the green light when all four checks below are true.
What you will be able to see or do: Confidence that the move was purely cosmetic
How you check it works: Tests green (same count as before) - app starts and a full run works - the command-line replay runs clean - the product behaves identically to before

Feature (plain name): We teach the AI our standards once
What it means and why it matters: Instead of you re-explaining how things should be built every time we write the rules down as skills the AI reads automatically. From here on every line of new code is written in a safer language and tested before it is trusted.
What you will be able to see or do: A set of written house rules the AI follows every time
How you check it works: Walk the steps - done when the rules load cleanly and a tiny sample change proves they are being followed

What it means and why it matters: We look at the best existing rulebooks the wider community uses and decide what to borrow versus what to write ourselves - no reinventing the wheel.
What you will be able to see or do: A clear decision on what we borrow versus build
How you check it works: The decision is written down in the progress log

What it means and why it matters: We install the test-first skill and the security skills so they are ready to use - the safety equipment goes on the shelf.
What you will be able to see or do: The quality and security skills installed and ready
How you check it works: All the installed skills load without error

What it means and why it matters: We write two plain rulebooks - one for the backend one for the frontend - covering naming structure and how the parts connect so everything is built the same way every time.
What you will be able to see or do: Two house-rule skills the AI reads automatically
How you check it works: Both rulebooks load and cover naming structure and layout

What it means and why it matters: We switch on the stricter language tooling and the test runner and lay out where tests live so mistakes get caught before the app even runs.
What you will be able to see or do: Tooling that catches mistakes early
How you check it works: The strict settings build the test runner runs and the test folders mirror the code

Feature (plain name): Point the AI at the rules
What it means and why it matters: We wire the project guide so the AI automatically opens the right rulebook for the right kind of work.
What you will be able to see or do: The AI reaching for the right rulebook on its own
How you check it works: Every link in the project guide opens the correct skill

What it means and why it matters: Confirm the standards are real - not just written down but actually followed on a tiny test change.
What you will be able to see or do: Confidence the rules will hold on every future change
How you check it works: Every skill loads without error - a small test-first change lands in correctly named files - all guide links resolve

Feature (plain name): We upgrade the code to a safer language
What it means and why it matters: We convert the existing code to a stricter language that flags whole classes of mistakes before the app runs - like spell-check for code. Nothing behaves differently - this is purely about catching errors earlier.
What you will be able to see or do: The same product built on safer foundations
How you check it works: Walk the steps - done when the safety checks and tests are green and the product behaves exactly as before

What it means and why it matters: We switch the backend into strict mode so the safety checks are at their most thorough from the start.
What you will be able to see or do: The backend running under strict safety checks
How you check it works: The strict settings build without complaint

Feature (plain name): Agree the shapes of our data
What it means and why it matters: We define the exact shape of the core things the system passes around (a session a question a briefing and so on) so every part agrees on what they look like - no surprises.
What you will be able to see or do: Clear agreed definitions for the core data
How you check it works: The definitions compile and the rest of the code uses them

What it means and why it matters: We convert the code from the outside in keeping every test green at each step so nothing breaks along the way.
What you will be able to see or do: The whole engine converted with tests staying green
How you check it works: Tests are green after each piece is converted

What it means and why it matters: Where the conversion reveals vague spots we tighten them up so there are no soft edges left.
What you will be able to see or do: Code with no vague or untyped gaps
How you check it works: No loose untyped spots remain in the converted code

What it means and why it matters: Confirm the upgrade was invisible to users - same product safer underneath.
What you will be able to see or do: Confidence the upgrade changed nothing visible
How you check it works: The safety build passes with no loose spots - tests green - app and command-line behave identically to before

Phase: 004 A proper engine room (Backend API v1)
Feature (plain name): We reshape the backend into clean layers
What it means and why it matters: We organise the backend behind a proper versioned doorway (an API) with clean layers so it can grow without becoming a tangled mess. The product does what it does today - it is just built to scale.
What you will be able to see or do: A backend organised to grow without turning into spaghetti
How you check it works: Walk the steps - done when every route follows the standard each piece is tested and behaviour is unchanged

Phase: 004 A proper engine room (Backend API v1)
What it means and why it matters: We list every service the backend offers and write down the official menu (the contract) - what you can ask for and what you get back.
What you will be able to see or do: A written menu of every backend service
How you check it works: A contract document lists every request its type and its response

Phase: 004 A proper engine room (Backend API v1)
What it means and why it matters: We build the shared plumbing every request flows through - error handling who-you-are context and a slot for the login check coming later.
What you will be able to see or do: Consistent handling for every request
How you check it works: Errors come back in one consistent shape and the shared plumbing works

Phase: 004 A proper engine room (Backend API v1)
Feature (plain name): Build each service in clean layers
What it means and why it matters: Each area is built as three small parts each with one job - a thin front desk the actual logic and the data access - and every piece is written test-first.
What you will be able to see or do: Each service split into small single-job parts
How you check it works: Each service has tests written before its code and the routes work

Phase: 004 A proper engine room (Backend API v1)
What it means and why it matters: We arrange the tests to mirror the code so anyone can find the test for any piece instantly.
What you will be able to see or do: A test layout that mirrors the code
How you check it works: Small tests sit beside their code and bigger tests live in a matching tree

Phase: 004 A proper engine room (Backend API v1)
What it means and why it matters: Confirm the new structure is clean and that storage could be swapped later without rewriting the logic.
What you will be able to see or do: Confidence the backend is ready to grow
How you check it works: Every route follows the standard - each service was tested first - storage could be swapped without touching the logic

Feature (plain name): We move live data into a real database
What it means and why it matters: Today everything is saved as loose files on disk - fine for one tester not for real companies. We move the live data (organisations users and sessions) into a proper database so multiple people can use it safely and nothing is lost on restart.
What you will be able to see or do: Live data held safely in a real database
How you check it works: Walk the steps - done when the database builds from scratch in one command and data survives a restart

What it means and why it matters: We choose the proven tool for managing the database and record the choice so the reasoning is on file.
What you will be able to see or do: A chosen database toolkit on the record
How you check it works: The choice is written down in the progress log

What it means and why it matters: We create the first tables - organisations users sessions a pointer to run history and a placeholder for invitations - as a proper versioned change not a hand-edit.
What you will be able to see or do: The core tables created cleanly
How you check it works: The database builds itself from empty with a single command

Feature (plain name): Plug the backend into the database
What it means and why it matters: We connect the backend to the database and swap session and user storage over - behind the same doorway so the logic above does not change.
What you will be able to see or do: The backend reading and writing the database
How you check it works: The services are unchanged and now read and write the database

What it means and why it matters: We make it possible for any teammate to spin up the database locally with one documented command.
What you will be able to see or do: A database any teammate can start in one step
How you check it works: A teammate can start the database from the written command

What it means and why it matters: Confirm data now truly persists - create something restart and watch it still be there.
What you will be able to see or do: Confidence real data is safe across restarts
How you check it works: Database builds from clean in one command - a session survives a server restart - tests green and a teammate can start the DB

What it means and why it matters: We give Sero a real front door: people register and log in with strong password protection. Because this is an HR product signing up also creates their company so their data belongs to the organisation not a lone person.
What you will be able to see or do: A secure register-and-login front door
How you check it works: Walk the steps - done when signup creates a company and two companies cannot see each other

What it means and why it matters: We finalise the tables for companies people sign-in methods and invitations so the front door has everything it needs.
What you will be able to see or do: The tables behind accounts finalised
How you check it works: The tables exist with every field they need

Feature (plain name): Register and login with safe passwords
What it means and why it matters: We build register and login and store passwords using a strong scramble so the real password is never kept - even we cannot see it.
What you will be able to see or do: Working register and login with safe passwords
How you check it works: Passwords are stored scrambled and the raw password is never kept

Feature (plain name): Keep people signed in and guard the doors
What it means and why it matters: We issue a secure pass on login and put a guard on protected pages so logged-out visitors are turned away.
What you will be able to see or do: Protected pages that turn away logged-out visitors
How you check it works: A protected page is refused when you are logged out

What it means and why it matters: Signing up creates the organisation and makes that first person the owner and every piece of data is fenced to their company.
What you will be able to see or do: A company created automatically on signup
How you check it works: Registering creates both a person and their company with that person as owner

What it means and why it matters: Confirm the front door is real and that companies are properly walled off from each other.
What you will be able to see or do: Confidence each company is sealed off from the rest
How you check it works: Signup creates a person and a company - login works and logged-out access is refused - two companies cannot see each others data

Feature (plain name): We build the app real customers use
What it means and why it matters: This is the visible payoff: a real customer-facing app with register and login wired to the secure backend. Your current internal screens stay as the admin tool - this new app is the product people sign up to.
What you will be able to see or do: A real app a customer can sign up to and use
How you check it works: Walk the steps - done when you can register a company in the browser log in and stay logged in

What it means and why it matters: We create the new customer app and its build setup - the shell the customer experience lives in.
What you will be able to see or do: The new customer app up and running
How you check it works: The app builds and runs

What it means and why it matters: We build the actual screens a new customer sees - register and login.
What you will be able to see or do: Register and login screens a customer can use
How you check it works: Both screens appear correctly

Feature (plain name): Wire the screens to the backend
What it means and why it matters: We connect those screens to the secure backend so signing up and logging in really work and the app remembers you.
What you will be able to see or do: Screens that really sign you up and log you in
How you check it works: Register a new company in the browser log in and reach the signed-in screen

What it means and why it matters: We add a minimal home screen that shows the company name and a button to start a prep run - enough to prove the loop end to end.
What you will be able to see or do: A home screen showing your company
How you check it works: The home screen shows your company name and a start button

What it means and why it matters: Confirm the whole loop works in a real browser the way a customer would experience it.
What you will be able to see or do: Confidence a real customer can sign up and use it
How you check it works: Register and log in in the browser and see the home screen - a refresh keeps you logged in - logout returns you to login

Feature (plain name): We make it safe for real HR data
What it means and why it matters: Before any real company puts staff data in we do a dedicated safety pass: protect peoples personal data and make sure our AI keys can never leak. Automated checks are the floor not the finish line - a human expert signs off before real data flows.
What you will be able to see or do: A product safe to trust with real staff data
How you check it works: Walk the steps - done when the checks are green keys cannot leak and a human expert has signed off

What it means and why it matters: We install the security skills and run their checks fixing anything they flag until they are all green.
What you will be able to see or do: A clean bill of health from the security checks
How you check it works: The security checks are all green

What it means and why it matters: We map everywhere personal and HR data lives and lock it down - access fenced by company and role data encrypted in transit and kept out of logs.
What you will be able to see or do: Personal data fenced by company and role
How you check it works: Trying to reach another company or roles data is blocked

What it means and why it matters: We prove our AI keys live only on the server and never reach a customers browser a response or a log file - this is critical.
What you will be able to see or do: AI keys that never leave the server
How you check it works: No key appears in the app a response or a log anywhere

What it means and why it matters: A security-literate human reviews the whole thing and signs off on the record - because for HR data green checks alone are not enough.
What you will be able to see or do: A named experts sign-off on the record
How you check it works: A named human expert has reviewed and signed off in the progress log

What it means and why it matters: This is the final gate before real data flows. Confirm all four checks below - and do not treat the automated greens as enough on their own.
What you will be able to see or do: Confidence it is genuinely safe to onboard a real company
How you check it works: Security checks green - no key leaks anywhere - personal data access is fenced by company and role - a named expert has signed off
